schedule_weeks.milestone: Final eval grid: 3 seeds, bootstrap CIs, Pareto plots; optional 30B-A3B-FP8 cross-model stretch if budget/time.
schedule_weeks.kill_gate: Locked S1-S3 met with CIs; else invoke Fallback A/B framing.

schedule_weeks.milestone: Analysis, figures, ablation tables, writing; document negatives honestly; freeze results.
schedule_weeks.kill_gate: All pre-registered claims resolved (supported or reported as negative).

schedule_weeks.milestone: Buffer: paper polish, reviewer-proofing, contingency reruns / stragglers.
schedule_weeks.kill_gate: Submission-ready by deadline; ~106 GPU-h contingency untouched or spent on 30B-A3B stretch.

plan.week1_kill_gate: By end of Week 1 on Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking over ~200 swap-sensitive VisualSwap instances (eager attention, per-head capture, BYTE-IDENTICAL re-exam string at the SAME absolute token position, image content = re-encoded I_b held fixed): GO iff (i) self-vs-oracle swap-detection accuracy gap >=15pp, AND (ii) the ROLE main effect survives position+boundary controls — condition C2 (user-role re-exam text injected with the image held at its ORIGINAL far-back slot, NO re-insertion) yields a visual-attention surge and accuracy gain whose instance-bootstrap 95% CI excludes 0 vs C1 (self/assistant mid-decode) AND is >=40% of the full-oracle C4 (image re-inserted recently) gain, AND boundary-only C3 (assistant-role at turn boundary, image far-back) does NOT reproduce C2's gain, AND the position-sweep slope does not account for C2. NO-GO / FALSIFY iff C2-C1 CI includes 0, OR C2 gain <20% of C4-C1 while C4 recovers, OR C3 matches C2 -> the surge is image-recency/position/boundary, not role/provenance -> stop building the role-gated machinery and execute the Fallback. This is the cheapest decisive test (~10-15 GPU-h for the pivotal C2-vs-C4 sub-contrast; ~72 GPU-h for the full P1 block including reproduction and sweep) and it gates all downstream work.
plan.fallback_if_mechanism_fails: Two-tier, pre-committed. FALLBACK A (role falsified but a training-free trigger still works — F2 via C3 surge or sweep): reframe from 'role/provenance gating' to the empirically-winning variable. If C3 surges -> the mechanism is TURN-BOUNDARY re-anchoring / attention-sink reset; the fix becomes 'inject a boundary marker at re-look steps, gated per-instance'. If the position sweep drives it -> the mechanism is IMAGE-RECENCY (IKOD 2508.03469); the fix becomes 're-prefill / re-position the visual KV entries at re-look steps'. Either remains a training-free, targeted, mechanism-grounded fix that still beats naive global-2x on collateral and still uses the per-instance gate — publishable as 'boundary-reset / recency gating for the re-examination illusion'. FALLBACK B (no clean single gate variable, or H2/H3 also weak, or F1): pivot to a rigorous MECHANISTIC CHARACTERIZATION paper — the first head-level, matched-content dissociation of what does and does NOT drive the re-examination surge (role vs position vs boundary vs instruction content), the position-sweep recency curve, Gaze-Heads-style localization with a role dimension, and the STRONGEST training-free targeted fix regardless of the exact gate variable, benchmarked Pareto-style against global-2x / CAST / DMAS with the collateral-on-swap-invariant metric. This is ICLR-viable as a mechanism+diagnosis contribution even if the specific 'role' hypothesis returns negative, because no prior work (2605.15864 coarse layer-avg; 2606.14703 no role dim; CAST/DMAS no dissociation) performs the matched-content head-level dissociation.
plan.scoop_verdict: NOT fully scooped, but the novelty is a CONJUNCTION resting substantially on ONE surviving, mechanism-contingent axis. Every OTHER constituent is independently published as training-free work: head-level visual-attention steering (CAST 2605.04641, DMAS 2602.21704, VisFlow/dual-level 2506.12609, Gaze Heads 2606.14703), per-token adaptive steering strength (TLVS 2606.07647), a training-free selective-attention inference fix for the visual-attention/correctness disconnect (Seeing but Not Believing 2510.17771), and — most dangerously — the phenomenon paper's OWN global 2x visual-attention amplification PoC (2605.15864 Tab.9, +18.2% Thinking, 'left to future work'). The genuinely UN-scooped conjunction is: [re-examination-illusion target] x [role/provenance mechanism, user-vs-self] x [per-instance WHETHER-to-steer gate] x [matched-content role-vs-position-vs-boundary HEAD-LEVEL dissociation]. The matched-content dissociation has NO prior overlap and is the true novelty anchor. HONEST residual scoop-risk ~0.5: the surviving axis is confounded by (a) image-RECENCY — the oracle re-inserts I_b recently while the self-probe leaves the image far-back, and IKOD 2508.03469 shows monotonic visual-attention decay, so an apparent 'role' effect could be pure recency; and (b) turn-boundary sink-reset. If the Week-1 dissociation shows the surge is positional or boundary-driven, the contribution collapses toward 'yet another training-free head-steer + gate on VisualSwap' (CAST/DMAS/TLVS/VisFlow territory) unless reframed via Fallback A/B. Two further honesty caveats: the training-free fix can only reweight STALE far-back visual KV, so its recovery likely hits a CEILING below the +51pp oracle; and the primary 'beats global without collateral' claim is unproven until we FIRST show global-2x actually incurs collateral (2605.15864 reports only gains). VRRL (2607.02490), Look-Back (2507.03019), BUS (2607.07361) are RL/training solutions to the SAME symptom — complementary and useful as motivation ('they think only training can fix this'), not scooping. Verdict: GO, but the paper lives or dies on the Week-1 matched-content dissociation and on demonstrating global-steering collateral. All arxiv IDs above verified this session via export.arxiv.org; models (Qwen3-VL-8B-Thinking, Qwen2.5-VL-7B-Instruct) confirmed non-gated on HF; VisualSwap dataset confirmed to exist (github.com/visualswap/visualswap) though HF access returned 401 and must be secured Week-0.
remaining_risks: Image-recency confound (DOMINANT): the user-turn oracle re-inserts I_b recently (R_multi=M(I_b,[Q,R_a,U])) while the self-probe keeps the image far-back; IKOD 2508.03469 shows monotonic visual-attention decay. An apparent 'role' effect may be pure recency. Mitigated by C2 (image held far-back) but the training-free fix can only reweight stale far-back KV -> restoration ceiling likely below the +51pp oracle.

Collateral-headroom risk: 2605.15864 reports only GAINS from global-2x, never collateral. If global steering is near-harmless on swap-invariant, H3 has no headroom -> the localized+gated complexity is unjustified -> must reframe (F3).

Scale-transfer: headline effects (3x, ~60% drop, +51pp) are on 235B/32B/28B Thinking models that DO NOT fit 4xL40; must reproduce >=15pp gap at 8B; 30B-A3B needs FP8 + multi-GPU sharding (no NVLink -> slow) as escalation only.

Statistical power: 800 pairs, math-only; per-model swap-sensitive subset after holding out a gate-train split may be a few hundred; claiming 'no degradation' with tight CIs is hard -> bootstrap CIs, held-out source-benchmark splits, pool the two primary models if N is thin.

Head-set generalization: role-conditioned heads may be model-specific (localized on 8B may not transfer to 30B-A3B) -> budget per-model localization; keep cross-model as a stretch claim, not load-bearing.

Grounding-head redundancy (Dual-Pathway 2605.13156): components are redundant and flip polarity between correct/hallucinating outputs; a small fixed head set may be unstable -> head-set-size ablation required.

Data access: HF visualswap/VisualSwap returned HTTP 401 this session (possibly gated) -> Week-0 secure via HF request/email authors, else reconstruct from public source benchmarks via download_data.py; timeline must not block on release.

Baseline fairness: CAST/DMAS have NO public code -> reimplement (~1-2 GPU-days engineering each; DMAS vector-DB is heaviest); running them on VisualSwap (OOD for them) may look artificially weak -> sanity-check on POPE home turf first and report both, or demote to cited-only if reimpl stalls.
Gate overfitting to VisualSwap math-only shortcuts -> held-out source-benchmark splits + swap-invariant negatives; report surface-feature-only baseline for the gate.

Eager-attention (non-FlashAttention) needed for capture+steering raises latency/memory -> measure real per-instance cost in Week 1 and size the eval grid to ~300-500 GPU-h (naive full grid ~800+ overruns); ~106 GPU-h contingency reserved.

Novelty-erosion by TLVS 2606.07647 (per-token steering) and VisFlow 2506.12609 (token+head intervention): read both in full before locking the 'per-instance gate' claim; if either already gates by a visual-sensitivity signal, sharpen the claim to role/provenance conditioning + re-look-step targeting, which none address.
